subject: Social Science (History)
chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

key_points: Nationalism in India emerged through political awareness, economic criticism, and social reform movements
Indian leaders criticized colonial economic policies such as the Drain of Wealth
The Partition of Bengal in 1905 triggered mass political protests across India
The nationalist movement evolved from moderate constitutional methods to mass political struggles
Gandhi transformed the freedom struggle into a nationwide mass movement
The Quit India Movement demanded immediate British withdrawal from India
examples: Dadabhai Naoroji explained the economic exploitation of India through the Drain of Wealth theory
Tilak inspired nationalism through slogans demanding Swaraj
Gandhi's Salt March challenged British monopoly over salt
Civil Disobedience Movement involved breaking colonial laws peacefully
Quit India Movement mobilized people across the country demanding independence
important_terms: Drain of Wealth Theory – Economic theory explaining transfer of Indian wealth to Britain
Satyagraha – Gandhi's philosophy of truth and nonviolent resistance
Mass Movement – Movement involving large participation of common people
Quit India Movement – Nationwide protest demanding British withdrawal in 1942
Partition of India – Division of India and Pakistan in 1947
exam_tips: Explain causes, events, and consequences of major movements
Use examples of leaders and their contributions
Compare early nationalism with Gandhian mass movements
Write answers with historical reasoning rather than only memorized facts
Mention important years like 1905 (Partition of Bengal), 1930 (Salt March), and 1942 (Quit India Movement)
